english: minister, a distinction not quite intelligible. — dmág-blon (dmag blon) military, yúl-blon (yul blon) civil officer Cs. — naṅ-blon (nang blon) 1. v. above ṗyi-naṅ (phyi nang) etc. 2. Lh. country-judge.
bha (bha), sometimes written for བ་, either from ignorance, or in order to appear learned, as is also དྷ་ for ད་, and so forth.
bhá-ga (bha ga) Ssk. the female genitals, Pth.

bhá-ra-ta (bha ra ta), bhá-ra-tai dúm-bu (bha ra ta'i dum bu), bhár-ta (bhar ta), bár-dha (bar dha), Ssk. भरतखण्ड country between Lanka and the Sumeru, viz. Hindustan; also North-India, Mil. and elsewh.
bha-lad (bha lad), Urd. ولایت, Beng. belati, 'a far distant country', = ṗi-liṅ (phi ling), for Europe.
bhaṅ-ge (bhang ge) W., Ssk. भङ्गा, hemp.
dba (dba') 1. Lex. = ꜩe-sa (rje sa) reverence, respect, obs. 2. (or rba (rba)) = the following.

dba-klóṅ (dba' klong) Glr., rba-kloṅ (rba klong) Mil., Dzl., dba-rlábs (dba' rlabs) wave, billow; rba-skya (rba skya) whitish waves Mil.; dba-tsúb (dba' tsub) surge, roar, turmoil of waves Cs.; dba-byi (dba' byi) water-rat?
dbág-pa (dbag pa), pf. dbags (dbags) Sch., v. dbog-pa (dbog pa) and bag-pa (bag pa).

dbaṅ (dbang) (*waṅ* (wang), vulg. *'aṅ* ('ang)) 1. might, power, potency, blón-po dbaṅ čés-pas (blon po dbang ches pas) because the minister was very potent Glr.; dbaṅ dge-ọdún-la ɣtad (dbang dge 'dun la gtad) Glr., not only: 'he granted great privileges to the priesthood' Sch., but: he invested it with magisterial power and jurisdiction; rarely used of physical power or strength S.g.; bsdad-dbaṅ-med (bsdad dbang med) it is not in my power to stay Thgy.; búm-pa ǰó-moi yin-te dbaṅ ma mčís-so (bum pa jo mo'i yin te dbang ma mchis so) as the pitcher belongs to my mistress, I have no power over it, I have not to dispose of it Dzl.; sdod-dbaṅ-méd-par (sdod dbang med par) having no strength, not being able to wait (from eagerness, avidity etc.) Glr.; dbaṅ-méd (dbang med), prob. sdod (sdod) to be supplied (if the text be correct), this won't do so any longer Glr.; (raṅ-)dbaṅ-méd-du ((rang) dbang med du) or par (par) involuntarily, not being able to help it, e.g. to weep, rejoice, believe, Mil.: dbaṅ-méd-du mči-ma ọcór-du ǰúg-pa (dbang med du mchi ma 'chor du 'jug pa) to make one weep; dbaṅ-méd-du ọcól-ba (dbang med du 'chol ba) to make a person powerless, to force by absolute power Glr.; dbáṅ-du gyúr-ba (dbang du gyur ba) to get into another's power, to be overpowered Tar.; ọdód-pai ('dod pa'i) to get into the power of the passions, to be led away by them Dzl.; dbáṅ-du gyúr-pa (dbang du gyur pa) seems also sometimes to mean: he who has brought every thing into his power (?), along with nyon-moṅs-pa-méd-pa (nyon mongs pa med pa) and sems-rnam-par-gról-ba (sems rnam par grol ba); dbáṅ-du sdúd-pa (dbang du sdud pa) to reduce under one's power Pth.; snyíṅ-rǰe (snying rje) to make the principle of mercy one's own, to practise it freely Glr., (where ọdú-ba ('du ba) stands) ọgró-ba ('gro ba) to comprise all beings, Glr.; dbáṅ-du byéd-pa (dbang du byed pa) id.; dbaṅ byéd-pa (dbang byed pa) c. la (la), 1. to rule over, to govern, frq. 2. to possess, bdág-gis dbaṅ byar méd-pa (bdag gis dbang byar med pa) what one does not possess Thgy. — dban-sgyúr-ba (dban sgyur ba) c. la (la), to govern, to rule, frq.; dbaṅ grúb-pa (dbang grub pa) id. seldom. — dbaṅ skúr-ba (dbang skur ba) v. skur-ba (skur ba) and dbaṅ (dbang), 2. *waṅ táṅ-če* (wang tang che) W. to make efforts, to exert one's self, also = the next. — dbaṅ zá-ba (dbang za ba) to offer violence Dzl. 240, 3. — dbáṅ-gis (dbang gis) like a postposition, by, by means of, in virtue of, in consequence of, e.g. lás-kyi (las kyi) of former actions Glr.; ṅa-rgyál-gyi dbáṅ-gis (nga rgyal gyi dbang gis) from or in consequence of pride Tar. — 2. more especially in mythology, dbaṅ bču (dbang bcu) Dzl. 202, 14, also stobs-bču (stobs bcu) Trigl. 8, 6; Gyatch. II, 46, Burn. II, 781 seqq. 1. the ten powers of knowledge of Buddha, v. Köpp. I, 437 seqq. 2. in later times ɣżan rǰés-su ądzin-pai dbaṅ bču (gzhan rjes su 'dzin pa'i dbang bcu) ten powers tending to the benefit of others are ascribed to the Bodhisattva, Thgy.: tsé-la dbáṅ-ba (tshe la dbang ba) (respecting this form v. below) power over the length of one's own life; séms-la dbáṅ-ba (sems la dbang ba) power according to one's own pleasure to enter into any meditation; yo-byád-la (yo byad la) to shower down provisions for the support of creatures; lás-la (las la) to mitigate the punishments for their sins; skyé-ba-la (skye ba la) to effect one's own re-birth in the external world, without danger of being infected by its sin; mós-pa-la (mos pa la) at pleasure to change one object into another; smón-lam-la (smon lam la) to see every prayer for the welfare of others fulfilled; rdzu-ọṗrúl-la (rdzu 'phrul la) to exhibit wonderful feats for bring-
